p.em: Intel® 64 and IA-32 Architectures Optimization Reference

div.p: Data alignment ensures that memory accesses do not cross hardware boundaries (Cache Lines, DRAM Pages). While padding increases memory footprint by 14.2% in this instance, it results in a deterministic latency reduction of 2x-4x during heavy SIMD processing by eliminating unaligned access stall cycles and allowing the hardware prefetcher to work more effectively.

ul.li: Unpadded: Particle #3 will cross cache line (Byte 56-84).
Padded: Every particle is aligned to cache line start or midpoint.
Packed (SSE): movups used. Handles unaligned loads with throughput penalty.
Aligned (SSE): movaps enabled. Atomic 128-bit load from memory to XMM registers.
AVX Note: Requires 32-byte alignment for vmovaps (256-bit).

g.text: Compiler adds 4B padding to enforce 16B struct size
